title: Season of Migration to the North
author: Tayeb Salih

setting.note: A small village on the great bend of the Nile in northern Sudan, in Salih's own home country near Karmakol and the town of Ad-Dabbah. The narrator comes back from years of study in England to the palms, the water-wheels and the river of his childhood, and finds a stranger, Mustafa Sa'eed, whose buried life in 1920s London shadows the whole book. The map runs along the Dongola reach of the Nile and south to Khartoum, with one deliberate excursion north to the England of the flashback.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Tayeb Salih, first published in Arabic (Mawsim al-Hijra ila al-Shamal) in 1966. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.translation: Denys Johnson-Davies's English translation (1969) is itself in copyright; none of it is stored or reproduced here.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.

palette.note: The blue-green of the Nile that runs through every scene and closes the book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
